title: Bodily Autonomy and Consent
content: You have the right to make decisions about your own body, including when and with whom to be intimate. Consent must be clear, enthusiastic, and can be withdrawn at any time.

title: Privacy and Confidentiality
content: All healthcare services, including SRHR, must keep your information private. This is especially important for young people seeking sensitive care.

title: Access to Youth-Friendly Services
content: You have the right to access healthcare that is non-judgmental, affordable, and accessible, regardless of your age or marital status.

title: Freedom from Violence
content: You have the right to live a life free from violence, including physical, emotional, and sexual abuse. Support is available in Kenya if you need it.

title: Right to Information
content: You have the right to accurate, evidence-based, and youth-friendly information about your sexual and reproductive health.

title: Freedom from Stigma
content: You deserve to seek care without being judged or stigmatized because of your choices, identity, or health status.

title: Sexual Health Rights
content: This includes the right to explore your sexuality safely and make informed decisions about your sexual health.

title: Right to Choice
content: The right to decide if, when, and how many children to have, and access to the necessary services to achieve that.

title: Dignity in Care
content: Everyone has the right to be treated with dignity and respect by healthcare providers, regardless of their background.

title: Empowerment & Agency
content: You have the power to advocate for your own needs and participate in decisions that affect your health.

title: Safe Working Environments
content: SRHR also covers the right to a safe environment free from harassment, especially for young workers.

title: Advocacy Rights
content: You can organize with other youth to advocate for better SRHR policies and services in Kenya.

title: Inclusive Rights
content: Discrimination based on disability, ethnicity, or any other status is illegal and a violation of your human rights.

title: Legal Protection
content: Understanding the Kenyan constitution and laws that protect your reproductive rights.

title: Emergency Care
content: The right to receive immediate care in case of emergencies, such as after an assault or a health crisis.
